contents: (** A wild category is pointed if the initial and terminal object are the same. *)
(** The zero morphism between objects [a] and [b] of a pointed category [A] is the unique morphism that factors through the zero object. *)

Definition zero_morphism {A : Type} `{IsPointedCat A} {a b : A} : a $-> b
  := (mor_initial _ b) $o (mor_terminal a _).

Context {A : Type} `{IsPointedCat A} {a b c : A}
    (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> c).

Definition cat_zero_source (h : zero_object $-> a) : h $== zero_morphism
    := (mor_initial_unique _ _ _)^$ $@ (mor_initial_unique _ _ _).

Definition cat_zero_target (h : a $-> zero_object) : h $== zero_morphism
    := (mor_terminal_unique _ _ _)^$ $@ (mor_terminal_unique _ _ _).
(** We show the last two arguments so that end points can easily be specified. *)
Definition cat_zero_l : zero_morphism b c $o f $== zero_morphism a c.

goals.conclusion: zero_morphism b c $o f $== zero_morphism a c
zero_morphism b c $o f $== zero_morphism a c
mor_terminal a zero_object $-> mor_terminal b zero_object $o f

contents: Definition cat_zero_r : g $o zero_morphism a b $== zero_morphism a c.

goals.conclusion: g $o zero_morphism a b $== zero_morphism a c
g $o zero_morphism a b $== zero_morphism a c
mor_initial zero_object c $== g $o mor_initial zero_object b

contents: (** Any morphism which factors through an object equivalent to the zero object is homotopic to the zero morphism. *)

Definition cat_zero_m `{!HasEquivs A} (be : b $<~> zero_object)
    : g $o f $== zero_morphism a c.

goals.conclusion: g $o be^-1$ $o (be $o f) $== zero_morphism a c
g $o be^-1$ $o mor_terminal a zero_object $== zero_morphism a c

contents: (** We make the last two arguments explicit so that end points can easily be specified. We had to do this again, since the section encapsulated the previous attempt. *)
(** A functor is pointed if it preserves the zero object. *)
(** Here is an alternative constructor using preservation of the zero object. This requires more structure on the categories however. *)
(** Pointed functors preserve the zero object up to isomorphism. *)

Lemma pfunctor_zero {A B : Type} (F : A -> B)
  `{IsPointedCat A, IsPointedCat B, !HasEquivs B,
    !Is0Functor F, !Is1Functor F, !IsPointedFunctor F}
  : F zero_object $<~> zero_object.
(** Pointed functors preserve the zero morphism up to homotopy *)

Lemma fmap_zero_morphism {A B : Type} (F : A -> B)
  `{IsPointedCat A, IsPointedCat B, !HasEquivs B,
    !Is0Functor F, !Is1Functor F, !IsPointedFunctor F} {a b : A}
  : fmap F (zero_morphism a b) $== zero_morphism (F a) (F b).

goals.conclusion: fmap F (zero_morphism a b) $== zero_morphism (F a) (F b)
fmap F (zero_morphism a b) $== zero_morphism (F a) (F b)

fmap F (mor_initial zero_object b) $o fmap F (mor_terminal a zero_object) $==
zero_morphism (F a) (F b)
?Goal $o fmap F (mor_terminal a zero_object) $== zero_morphism (F a) (F b)

mor_initial (F zero_object) (F b) $o fmap F (mor_terminal a zero_object) $==
zero_morphism (F a) (F b)
mor_initial (F zero_object) (F b) $o ?Goal $== zero_morphism (F a) (F b)

mor_initial (F zero_object) (F b) $o mor_terminal (F a) (F zero_object) $==
zero_morphism (F a) (F b)

contents: (** Opposite category of a pointed category is also pointed. *)
Instance ispointedcat_op {A : Type} `{IsPointedCat A} : IsPointedCat A^op.
